title: Mildly Sweet Uruguayan Cream Flan
description: A dense and nostalgic Uruguayan dessert featuring clotted cream and a caramelized sugar crust, with a floral and mildly sweet flavor profile, this dish is also anti-inflammatory friendly due to the use of turmeric and ginger in the caramel sauce.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium saucepan, combine the sugar, turmeric, ginger, and salt, and heat over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar has dissolved.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the caramel is a deep amber color.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and slowly pour in the water, being careful not to splash the hot caramel.
In a large bowl, whisk together the clotted cream, eggs, and vanilla extract.
Strain the caramel sauce into the bowl with the cream mixture and whisk until well combined.
Pour the mixture into 6 ramekins or small baking dishes.
Place the ramekins in a large baking dish and add hot water to come halfway up the sides of the ramekins.
Bake for 45 minutes, or until the edges are set and the centers are still slightly jiggly.
Remove the ramekins from the water bath and let cool to room temperature, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or overnight.
